content: 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems can be restructured to treat agent-level environmental curiosity as a dynamically expanding 'episodic memory query' rather than a fixed intrinsic reward signal, letting agents prioritize learning only high-impact memory updates rather than overfilling storage with trivial novel observations. This reframing turns the problem of curiosity-driven exploration into a memory management task that aligns active learning directly with the agent's long-term planning goals.
